title: A summer camp packing list parents can actually use
excerpt: A practical checklist for parents who want to feel prepared without overpacking or missing the small things that matter.

sections.heading: Start with comfort and routine basics
sections.body: The most useful packing list usually begins with comfortable clothing, weather-appropriate layers, toiletries, sleep essentials, and any routine items your child depends on. This keeps the checklist practical instead of aspirational.

sections.heading: Pack for flexibility, not perfection
sections.body: Georgia weather can shift, and active camp days can get messy quickly. Extra socks, a light rain layer, refillable water bottles, and labeled personal items often matter more than packing trendy gear your child may never use.

sections.heading: Use the list as a confidence tool
sections.body: A strong packing list does more than help with logistics. It reduces parent stress and helps children feel more prepared for the experience ahead, especially if camp is new for them.

cta.heading: Browse Camp Dream GA programs if you are moving from planning into booking.
cta.body: The resource library is meant to help you prepare and choose confidently.
